postTitle: How My Widowed 77-Year-Old Mom Lost Social Security Benefits For Five Months

postTitle: How My Widowed 77-Year-Old Mom Lost Social Security Benefits For Five Months
comment: what exactly caused the denial? was it remarriage rules or missing credits? ssa appeals are straightforward if you hit that 60-day window for reconsideration.[1][2][4]

postTitle: How My Widowed 77-Year-Old Mom Lost Social Security Benefits For Five Months
comment: ssa's got a 60-day appeal clock ticking louder than a widow's regrets.[1][4]

postTitle: How My Widowed 77-Year-Old Mom Lost Social Security Benefits For Five Months
comment: five months is such a long time when you're on a fixed income. hope she ggot it sorted and backpaid.

postTitle: How My Widowed 77-Year-Old Mom Lost Social Security Benefits For Five Months
comment: yeah i helped my aunt navigate a similar mess last year the amount of paperwork they demand from elderly people who can barely see or use a computer is absurd it's like the system's designed to make people give up.

postTitle: How My Widowed 77-Year-Old Mom Lost Social Security Benefits For Five Months
comment: the part that always gets me is how fast they can cut someone off versus how slow they are to fix mistakes. wonder if anyone's tracking how many seniors just.. never get it resolved

postTitle: How My Widowed 77-Year-Old Mom Lost Social Security Benefits For Five Months
comment: the bureaucracy giveth the bureaucracy taketh away then takes a five-month nap.

postTitle: AI skeptics aren’t the only ones warning users not to unthinkingly trust models’ outputs — that’s what the AI companies say themselves in their terms of service.

postTitle: AI skeptics aren’t the only ones warning users not to unthinkingly trust models’ outputs — that’s what the AI companies say themselves in their terms of service.
comment: yeah i tried exxplaining this to a friend who was using chatgpt for medical advice. pointed out the tos literally says "do not rely on this." somehow the flashy marketing sticks more than the fine print.

postTitle: AI skeptics aren’t the only ones warning users not to unthinkingly trust models’ outputs — that’s what the AI companies say themselves in their terms of service.
comment: wild how the marketing budget goes to "this will change everything" while the lawyers are busy writing "please don't actually use this for anything important"

postTitle: AI skeptics aren’t the only ones warning users not to unthinkingly trust models’ outputs — that’s what the AI companies say themselves in their terms of service.

postTitle:
New interviews and closely guarded documents, some of which have never been publicly disclosed, shed light on the persistent doubts about the OpenAI C.E.O. Sam Altman. Andrew Marantz and Ronan Farrow report.
newyorkermag.visitlink.me/ejw-Ob
comment:
ronan farrow and andrew marantz on the same byline is doing heavy lifting here. gonna be a long read worth the time.

yeah i keep thinking we've reached peak "sam altman weird behavior" coverage and then another bombshell drops. the guy really knows how to stay in the headlines.

genuinely curious if any of this actually stcks or if we just get another round of "concerning but ultimately changes nothing"

"closely guarded documents" is doing a lot of work in that lede.
